2604.27660.title: From Context to Skills: Can Language Models Learn from Context Skillfully?
2604.27660.paper_summary: Many real-world tasks require language models (LMs) to reason over complex contexts that exceed their parametric knowledge. This calls for context learning, where LMs directly learn relevant knowledge from the given context. An intuitive solution is inference-time skill augmentation: extracting the rules and procedures from context into natural-language skills. However, constructing such skills for context learning scenarios faces two challenges: the prohibitive cost of manual skill annotation for long, technically dense contexts, and the lack of external feedback for automated skill construction, since there is no automatic signal to tell whether a proposed skill is helpful. In this paper, we propose Ctx2Skill, a self-evolving framework that autonomously discovers, refines, and selects context-specific skills without human supervision or external feedback. At its core, a multi-agent self-play loop has a Challenger that generates probing tasks and rubrics, a Reasoner that attempts to solve them guided by an evolving skill set, and a neutral Judge that provides binary feedback. Crucially, both the Challenger and the Reasoner evolve through accumulated skills: dedicated Proposer and Generator agents analyze failure cases and synthesize them into targeted skill updates for both sides, enabling automated skill discovery and refinement. To prevent adversarial collapse caused by increasingly extreme task generation and over-specialized skill accumulation, we further introduce a Cross-time Replay mechanism that identifies the skill set achieving the best balance across representative cases for the Reasoner side, ensuring robust and generalizable skill evolution. The resulting skills can be plugged into any language model to obtain better context learning capability. Evaluated on four context learning tasks from CL-bench, Ctx2Skill consistently improves solving rates across backbone models.
2604.27660.paper_summary_zh: 許多現實世界的任務要求語言模型 (LMs) 在超出其參數知識的複雜上下文中進行推理。這需要上下文學習，其中 LMs 直接從給定的上下文中學習相關知識。一個直觀的解決方案是推理時的技能增強：將上下文中的規則和程序提取為自然語言技能。然而，為上下文學習場景構建這樣的技能面臨兩個挑戰：對於長且技術密集的上下文，手動技能標註的高昂成本，以及缺乏外部反饋以進行自動化技能構建，因為沒有自動信號告訴我們所提議的技能是否有幫助。在本文中，我們提出了 Ctx2Skill，一個自我演化的框架，能夠在沒有人工監督或外部反饋的情況下，自主發現、完善和選擇特定於上下文的技能。其核心是一個多代理自我對弈循環，其中有一個挑戰者生成探測任務和標準，一個推理者試圖根據不斷演變的技能集來解決這些任務，還有一個中立的評判者提供二元反饋。關鍵是，挑戰者和推理者都通過積累的技能進化：專門的提議者和生成器代理分析失敗案例，並將其綜合為針對雙方的技能更新，實現自動化的技能發現和完善。為了防止由於越來越極端的任務生成和過度專門化的技能積累所造成的對抗性崩潰，我們進一步引入了一種跨時間重播機制，該機制識別出在推理者方面達到最佳平衡的技能集，確保穩健且可泛化的技能演化。所產生的技能可以插入任何語言模型，以獲得更好的上下文學習能力。在 CL-bench 的四個上下文學習任務上進行評估，Ctx2Skill 一直提高各個基礎模型的解決率。
2604.27660.author: Shuzheng Si et.al.
2604.27660.authors: Shuzheng Si, Haozhe Zhao, Yu Lei, Qingyi Wang, Dingwei Chen, Zhitong Wang, Zhenhailong Wang, Kangyang Luo, Zheng Wang, Gang Chen, Fanchao Qi, Minjia Zhang, Maosong Sun
